results.identified_issue: The request has three real asks and one ambiguous one. Toolbar swap: gear -> topBarLeading, browse (+eye menu) -> topBarTrailing. 'Different voices': Kokoro's voices.bin already carries 11 speaker embeddings (0-10, af/am/bf/bm), so I exposed all 11 as a picker and threaded the chosen speaker through SpeechRenderer's cache key + render() so each voice caches separately and a switch just changes upcoming cards. Default stays speaker 5 (Adam) so existing listeners hear no change. 'Different models' is the ambiguous one: the app has exactly ONE neural model (Kokoro-82M) plus the always-available AVSpeechSynthesizer fallback. I interpreted 'models' as the voice ENGINE and added a real Kokoro-vs-System choice (system voice needs no 351MB download), which also gates the auto-download so a system-voice listener isn't billed the bandwidth. I did NOT fabricate a second neural model; the model section is per-model so a real second model would just add a row. 'Download status of each voice model' -> a Voice model section showing Installed / Downloading %/progress bar / Download button driven by KokoroModelStore. Rejected: forcing a re-render of the currently-playing card on a voice change (kept it next-card, like speed already is) to avoid a mid-sentence voice flip.
results.detailed_description: Four changes. (1) Toolbar sides swapped: the settings gear is now on the left and the question controls (browse + the show/hide-question eye menu) are on the right, as you asked. (2) Settings is no longer a single speed knob — it's now a proper speech screen with a Voice engine choice, a Voice picker, Reading speed, and a Voice model status section. (3) Voices: Kokoro already ships 11 built-in speakers inside its weights (US and UK, male and female), so you can now pick among all of them — Adam, Bella, Emma, George, and so on. The choice is baked into the render cache key, so each voice caches its own audio and switching just changes how the next cards sound; the default stays the voice Steelman shipped with. (4) Models + download status: I read 'different models' as choosing the voice ENGINE — the natural on-device Kokoro voice (a one-time 351 MB download) vs. the system voice (no download). Picking the system voice also stops the app from spending your data on the Kokoro download; switching back kicks it off. The new Voice model section shows Kokoro-82M's live state: a Download button when it hasn't started, a progress bar and percentage while it downloads, and an Installed checkmark with the size when it's ready. One honest caveat: there is only one neural model today (Kokoro-82M). I did not invent a second one — the model list is built per-model so a genuine second model would just add a row. If by 'different models' you meant adding a second neural TTS model (new weights, new download), that's a much bigger piece of work — tell me and I'll scope it. I couldn't run the app from this flow (a watcher builds and deploys the pushed commit), so I verified by reading: the engine/voice/speed all persist to UserDefaults, the speaker is threaded through every SpeechRenderer call site and the prefetch, and the player falls back to the system voice whenever Kokoro isn't chosen or isn't ready yet.
results.summary: Moved settings to the left and the question icon to the right, and built out Settings with a voice-engine choice, a voice picker, and live model download status

description: Settings should be actually be on the left and let's put the question icon on the right. Then settings should have more detail about the speech, text-to-speech. It should give us the option to choose different models and different voices. You should also handle all the download progress, download status of each voice model. Thank you.
